explanation: The LTL property states that globally (G), if the system is in 'StartUpMode', then it must remain in 'StartUpMode' (StartUpMode U) until 'initDone' becomes true, and in the very next state (X), the system transitions into 'SelfTestMode'. This accurately captures the requirement that the transition from 'StartUpMode' to 'SelfTestMode' shall occur once the configurations have been loaded and internal checking is terminated (represented by 'initDone'). The 'U' operator ensures that 'StartUpMode' persists until the condition is met, and 'X SelfTestMode' ensures the immediate transition upon satisfaction of 'initDone'.

The property states that globally (G), whenever the system is in 'Start-up Mode' (StartUpMode), it must eventually transition to 'Self test Mode' (SelfTestMode). Furthermore, this transition can only occur once 'the configurations have been loaded and the internal checking is terminated' (initDone). The 'until' operator (U) is used to capture this precise temporal relationship: '!SelfTestMode' must hold until 'SelfTestMode' becomes true, and at the exact moment 'SelfTestMode' becomes true, 'initDone' must also be true. This ensures that 'initDone' is a necessary condition for the system to enter 'SelfTestMode' from 'StartUpMode'.

The natural language statement "The transition from Start-up Mode to Self test Mode shall occur once the configurations have been loaded and the internal checking is terminated" implies a guaranteed sequence of events. First, if the system is in `StartUpMode`, it must eventually transition to `SelfTestMode`. Second, this transition to `SelfTestMode` can only happen after (or at the same time as) the condition `initDone` (configurations loaded and internal checking terminated) is met. The LTL formula `G (StartUpMode -> F (initDone & F SelfTestMode))` captures this by stating: if the system is currently in `StartUpMode`, then eventually a state must be reached where `initDone` is true, and from that very state, `SelfTestMode` must eventually become true. This effectively enforces the temporal ordering that `initDone` must precede or occur concurrently with the entry into `SelfTestMode`, and that the `SelfTestMode` will eventually be reached from `StartUpMode` under this condition. This formulation ensures that `SelfTestMode` cannot be entered before `initDone` has occurred in the context of the `StartUpMode`'s progression.

The natural language statement specifies a condition for the transition from 'Start-up Mode' to 'SelfTest Mode'. The phrase "shall occur once the configurations have been loaded and the internal checking is terminated" implies that the condition 'initDone' must be true at the precise moment this transition takes place. The LTL formula captures this by stating that globally (G), if the system is currently in 'StartUpMode' and in the next state (X) it transitions to 'SelfTestMode', then the 'initDone' condition must be true in the current state (i.e., at the time of the transition). This ensures that the transition only happens when 'initDone' is satisfied.

This LTL property states that globally, if the system is in 'Start-up Mode', it must eventually transition to 'SelfTest Mode'. Furthermore, this transition to 'SelfTest Mode' can only occur at a point in time where 'initDone' (indicating that configurations have been loaded and internal checking is terminated) is also true. The 'U' (Until) operator ensures that 'SelfTestMode' remains false until the specific condition ('initDone' and 'SelfTestMode' becoming true simultaneously) is met, thereby capturing the notion of a transition occurring 'once' the prerequisite condition is satisfied.

This LTL property specifies the required behavior for the transition from 'Start-up Mode' to 'SelfTest Mode'. It states that if the system is in 'StartUpMode', it must remain in 'StartUpMode' (or states where 'StartUpMode' is active) until 'initDone' (configurations loaded and internal checking terminated) becomes true. Crucially, when 'initDone' becomes true, in the very next state, the system must transition to 'SelfTestMode' and simultaneously cease to be in 'StartUpMode'. This captures the 'transition once' causality described in the natural language.

The LTL property ensures that globally, if the system is in 'Start-up Mode' (StartUpMode), it must eventually transition to 'Self-test Mode' (SelfTestMode). This transition is contingent upon the condition 'initDone' (configurations loaded and internal checking terminated) being met. The 'Until' operator (U) specifies that 'StartUpMode' must remain true until 'initDone' and 'SelfTestMode' both become true. This accurately captures that the transition to 'SelfTestMode' from 'StartUpMode' will occur once the initialization is complete. Implicitly, as 'StartUpMode' and 'SelfTestMode' are typically mutually exclusive states, 'StartUpMode' would cease to be true when 'SelfTestMode' becomes true.

The natural language describes a state transition: "The transition from Start-up Mode to Self test Mode shall occur once the configurations have been loaded and the internal checking is terminated." This means that while the system is in StartUpMode, it must eventually reach a point where initDone becomes true, and at that specific point, the system transitions into SelfTestMode. The LTL formula G (StartUpMode -> (StartUpMode U (initDone & X SelfTestMode))) captures this precise behavior. It states that globally (G), if the system is in StartUpMode, it must continue to be in StartUpMode until (U) the condition initDone becomes true, and at the next moment (X), the system enters SelfTestMode. This correctly models initDone as the trigger for the transition, ensuring that the system remains in StartUpMode until the condition is met and then moves to SelfTestMode in the subsequent state.

The natural language statement specifies a transition from 'Start-up Mode' to 'SelfTest Mode' that must occur 'once' a specific condition, 'initDone', is met. The LTL formula `G (StartUpMode -> (StartUpMode U (initDone & X SelfTestMode)))` captures this precise behavior. The outer `G` (Globally) ensures that this property holds at all times. The implication `StartUpMode -> ...` means that whenever the system enters `StartUpMode`, the subsequent condition must be met. The core of the translation is the `U` (Until) operator: `StartUpMode U (initDone & X SelfTestMode)`. This means that `StartUpMode` must continuously hold until the condition `(initDone & X SelfTestMode)` becomes true. When `(initDone & X SelfTestMode)` becomes true, it signifies two things: `initDone` is true at the current moment (the 'once' trigger), and `SelfTestMode` becomes true in the very next state (`X SelfTestMode`), effectively modeling the transition to `SelfTestMode` immediately after `initDone` is satisfied while in `StartUpMode`.

The natural language statement describes a mandatory transition from 'Start-up Mode' to 'Self test Mode', which is conditional upon 'initDone' (configurations loaded and internal checking terminated). The LTL property captures this by stating that globally, if the system is in 'StartUpMode', it must eventually transition to 'SelfTestMode', and this transition can only occur when 'initDone' is true. The 'U' (Until) operator ensures that 'SelfTestMode' remains false until the condition (initDone & SelfTestMode) becomes true. This means that 'SelfTestMode' can only become true if 'initDone' is also true at that exact moment, effectively making 'initDone' a prerequisite for the transition and ensuring that the transition eventually occurs.
